title: Configure watermarks against real skew
description: Measure out-of-order behavior per source and set watermark and lateness policy so windows fire correctly.
body: Use when wiring event-time windows over real sources. Do not copy a watermark delay from an example — every source has its own skew and idle behavior. Actions: sample the arrival-vs-event-time gap, set the watermark to cover typical lateness, add allowed lateness and a side output for stragglers, and handle idle partitions so they do not stall the watermark. Validation signals include windows firing on watermark advance and a near-zero dropped-late metric under normal skew. Risk boundary: an idle partition can freeze the global watermark and stop all output; if windows stop firing, escalate to idle-source handling before assuming a data outage.
